name: Shankha Conch
description: The Shankha Conch, a sacred marine shell, holds immense significance in Hindu mythology and tradition. Originating from the churning of the cosmic ocean (Samudra Manthan), it is believed to be one of the fourteen precious gems (ratnas) that emerged. The Shankha is closely associated with Lord Vishnu, often depicted holding it as one of his primary attributes. Its resonant sound, produced by blowing into the shell, is considered auspicious and is used to commence religious ceremonies, prayers, and rituals. The sound is believed to purify the environment, dispel negativity, and invoke divine blessings. Different types of Shankhas exist, each possessing unique characteristics and significance, such as the Dakshinavarti Shankha, which opens to the right and is considered particularly sacred and rare. The Shankha symbolizes creation, prosperity, and victory. It is also associated with the five elements (Panchabhuta) and represents the element of sound (Shabda). The conch's spiral shape is seen as a representation of the cosmic evolution and the cycle of life. Its presence in homes and temples is believed to bring peace, harmony, and spiritual upliftment. The Shankha's sound is also linked to the primordial sound 'Om,' the foundation of all creation. The Shankha serves as a powerful reminder of the divine presence and the interconnectedness of all things.
subtitle: Sacred shell of divine power and purity
origin: Churning of the Cosmic Ocean (Samudra Manthan)
currentLocation: Worshipped in temples and homes globally

keyMyths.title: Samudra Manthan
keyMyths.description: The Shankha emerged during the churning of the ocean by the Devas and Asuras, making it a divine artifact. It represents prosperity and is closely associated with Vishnu and Lakshmi.

keyMyths.title: Vishnu's Attribute
keyMyths.description: Lord Vishnu holds the Shankha as one of his main attributes, symbolizing his power and control over the universe. Its sound is believed to echo his divine presence.

sources.author: Roshen Dalal
